title: Cherry-Pick, Replay, Patch Equivalence, and Backports · Graph Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs cherry-pick, replay, patch equivalence, and backports from terse repository evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the multi-line backport and patch-equivalence record. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for multi-line backport and patch-equivalence record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select and order commits for cherry-pick using explicit revision traversal while distinguishing argument order from revision-walk order.
Handle merge mainline, empty, redundant, no-commit, signoff, and conflict continuation choices with release-policy evidence.
Use patch-id, cherry marks, range-diff, and tree comparisons to detect likely equivalent changes while naming heuristic limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Gita Cherry Pick Set Order · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — Case G72 is a review in a disposable repository. one differently named backport is patch-equivalent and should not be replayed twice. The learner must predict minus marks likely equivalent patches while plus marks changes absent from the target, run git cherry -v release security-fix, explain the parser fix is equivalent but the dependency-bound test remains target-specific work, test this changed condition: alter context without changing the logical patch and compare heuristic patch IDs with behavior, and preserve this recovery boundary: tag each target pre-backport tip under refs/recovery before sequencer operations.

Which action demonstrates Gita Cherry Pick Set Order without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select and order commits for cherry-pick using explicit revision traversal while distinguishing argument order from revision-walk order.

content.title: Evidence defense
